name: UNICEF USA
mission: Over eight decades, the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) has built an unprecedented global support system for the world's children. UNICEF relentlessly works day in and day out to deliver the essentials that give every child an equitable chance in life: health care and immunizations, safe water and sanitation, nutrition, education, emergency relief and more. UNICEF USA advances the global mission of UNICEF by rallying the American public to support the world's most vulnerable children. Together, we have helped save more children's lives than any other humanitarian organization.
theoryOfChange: UNICEF USA, through its Impact Fund for Children, has co-developed a 'Child-Lens Investment Framework'. This framework serves as a roadmap for investors to frame strategies and direct capital toward advancing child outcomes and minimizing child harm. It is described as a 'central guiding document' that includes core principles, a case for investment, an analysis of the global market landscape, and a description of the Child-Lens Investment ecosystem. Additionally, the Impact Fund reports impact through an 'Input/Output/Outcomes Framework' and by mapping each acceleration to UNICEF to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

grantsData.purpose: SEE PART V UNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN CENTRAL AMERICA AND THE CARIBBEAN, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF'S WORK IN EAST ASIA IS FOCUSED ON EMERGENCY RESPONSE, AS OVER 1.6 BILLION PEOPLE IN THE REGION HAVE BEEN AFFECTED BY NATURAL DISASTERS SINCE 2000. THE REGION IS ALSO SIGNIFICANTLY AFFECTED BY THE NEGATIVE IMPACTS OF CLIMATE CHANGE. CYCLONES, FLOODS AND DROUGHTS ARE MORE FREQUENT AND MORE INTENSE THAN EVER BEFORE. UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON MITIGATING HARM IN THE WAKE OF NATURAL DISASTERS AND STRENGTHENING THE REGION'S HEALTH, NUTRITION, SOCIAL PROTECTION, AND CHILD PROTECTION INFRASTRUCTURE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF IS WORKING TO REACH THE TENS OF MILLIONS OF CHILDREN ACROSS THE MIDDLE EAST AND NORTH AFRICA ARE HEAVILY IMPACTED BY CONFLICT, THE CLIMATE CHANGE CRISIS AND OTHER PROTRACTED EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING, FOR EXAMPLE, IN AFGHANISTAN, WHERE 28.3 MILLION PEOPLE, MORE THAN 65 PERCENT OF THE POPULATION, ARE PROJECTED TO BE IN NEED OF HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE IN 2023.
SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN SOUTH AMERICA, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF'S WORK IN SOUTH ASIA FOCUSES ON CHILD PROTECTION, EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING RELIEF FROM FLOODING IN PAKISTAN, EDUCATION, AND DELIVERY OF CRITICAL SUPPLIES TO COMBAT THE COVID-19 PANDEMIC.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF IS WORKING TO DELIVER HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE TO THOSE IMPACTED BY FOOD CRISES, SCREENING AND TREATING CHILDREN SUFFERING FROM MALNUTRITION AND PROVIDING SAFE WATER, SANITATION AND HYGIENE SERVICES TO COMMUNITIES WHERE THESE BASIC RESOURCES ARE IN SHORT SUPPLY, AMONG OTHER INTERVENTIONS.

judge_issues.message: The rationale states that the organization earns a GMG score of 70/100, but the assigned score is 70. This could be rephrased to avoid confusion.

The rationale states: "Alignment 31/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, education global cause area". This could be clearer by explicitly stating that the alignment score is 31 out of 50 due to a medium Muslim donor fit and the education global cause area.
Evaluation source domain 'charitywatch.org' is not a known trusted evaluator

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium alignment for Muslim donors seeking sadaqah-eligible opportunities in global education and health [6][7]. While it addresses urgent global causes, the alignment score of 31/50 reflects a balanced track record between broad international appeal and specific donor preferences [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: UNICEF USA demonstrates high impact through capacity building, maintaining a program expense ratio of 87.6% and a high data confidence level [1][3]. Its financial health is evidenced by a 97.0/100 rating from third-party evaluators, though it maintains a lean 2.8 months of working capital [1].

Efficient fundraising operations with a cost of only $0.12 per $1 raised

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects <cite id="13">verified outcomes</cite> and high transparency, though cost-per-beneficiary data is not consistently public.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: UNICEF USA uses a <cite id="14">strong theory of change</cite> focused on capacity building to end preventable child deaths.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that large-scale funding actually translates into reduced child mortality rates.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Global humanitarian needs for children consistently exceed available funding.

peer_comparison.differentiator: UNICEF USA is distinguished by its <cite id="8">extremely low fundraising cost</cite> compared to its peers.

strengths.detail: The charity maintains a <cite id="6">97/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of transparency.
UNICEF USA spends only <cite id="8">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
The organization maintains a <cite id="9">87.6% program expense ratio</cite>, directing the vast majority of funds to field operations.

strengths_deep_dive: UNICEF USA demonstrates institutional-grade governance with a <cite id="10">board of 20 members</cite>, of which <cite id="11">95% are independent</cite>. This oversight is paired with a <cite id="12">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level available. Their impact is further validated by <cite id="13">tracking specific outcome metrics</cite> and maintaining a <cite id="14">documented theory of change</cite> that guides their global health and nutrition interventions.
rich_narrative.summary: UNICEF USA serves as a critical fundraising and advocacy arm for the global United Nations Children's Fund, focusing on <cite id="1">ending preventable maternal and child deaths</cite>. The organization operates at a massive scale, reporting <cite id="2">$829,050,932 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. This funding supports essential programs in <cite id="3">health, nutrition, water, and education</cite> across more than 190 countries. While the organization is a global powerhouse, it recently faced a financial contraction, with <cite id="4">revenue declining to $720,404,955</cite> in the most recent filing. Despite this dip, UNICEF USA maintains <cite id="5">a perfect 100 score for Leadership</cite> and a <cite id="6">97/100 overall rating from Charity Navigator</cite>. Their operational model emphasizes capacity building, ensuring that aid is not just delivered but integrated into local systems. For donors, this represents an opportunity to support <cite id="7">large-scale humanitarian interventions</cite> that address the root causes of child mortality and poverty.
